system.description:
A set of travel documents presenting the party as a merchants' retinue. The seal is official-looking — almost. It uses a non-standard merchant cipher, the kind only a king's personal secretary would know how to forge.

Good enough to pass a half-asleep Chainwarden. Less convincing under close scrutiny.

Effect: When presented to a Chainwarden checkpoint guard, the bearer makes a Presence test. moderate effect+: the papers are accepted without question. weak effect: the reviewing officer notices the seal is irregular and becomes suspicious (+1 Interest toward questioning), but does not immediately refuse passage.

GM Notes

Received: Beat 3 (The Quiet Summons) — King Harald provides these papers to help the party slip out of Hrafnborg and avoid Chainwarden scrutiny. At the Hrafnborg departure checkpoint, the guard is half-asleep and waves them through. Save the tension for the Greypass Toll, where Eirik Greyhand examines them more carefully (Beat 4a).

Using the papers: When presented at a checkpoint, the bearer makes a Presence test (moderate difficulty). moderate effect+: accepted without question. weak effect: the officer notes the irregular seal ('This isn't the standard merchant cipher. Where did you get these?') and gains +1 suspicion, but does not refuse passage yet. Once a Chainwarden has noted the irregular seal, word may spread to subsequent checkpoints.

system.corruptionDescription: Using this artifact costs 1 temporary corruption.
